title: Authenticated Encryption with AES-GCM · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for authenticated encryption with aes-gcm and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit native, entropy, interoperability, side-channel, compliance, or production transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for versioned AES-GCM record envelope. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AEAD Seal and Open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication.
Design a crash-, concurrency-, rollback-, and replication-safe GCM nonce strategy within the construction limits.
Bind protocol version, tenant, object identity, algorithm suite, direction, and other non-secret context as canonical associated data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Crypto Go Aead Contract
steps.instruction:
Order the Crypto Go Aead Contract Cryptography and Go threat-to-evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Crypto Go Aead Contract.

content.text: A quiz cryptographic engineering team handles case 8222 during a post-quantum compatibility canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; preserve accessible status, non-color meaning, keyboard operation, error recovery, and secret-safe diagnostics; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use AEAD Seal and Open as atomic confidentiality-and-integrity operations and release plaintext only after successful authentication.

Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Crypto Go Gcm Failure Uniformity
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Crypto Go Gcm Failure Uniformity: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz cryptographic engineering team handles case 5980 during a credential migration window. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Collapse attacker-visible authentication failures while retaining safe internal classification and metrics.

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Crypto Go Gcm Failure Uniformity.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Collapse attacker-visible authentication failures while retaining safe internal classification and metrics.
